meta_title: Roofing in Roosevelt Gardens, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Roosevelt Gardens, FL. HVHZ-rated systems by CertainTeed & West Lake. Hurricane-ready replacements & repairs. Licensed, A+ BBB. Call today!
h1: Roosevelt Gardens' Most Trusted Roofing Contractor for Hurricane-Rated Replacements & Repairs
intro:
Roosevelt Gardens is a tight-knit community in the heart of Broward County where homeowners take pride in their properties — and with average home values around $240,000, protecting that investment starts at the top. Your roof is your home's first and most critical line of defense against South Florida's brutal storm seasons, and in a neighborhood where roughly 65% of homes were built before 1994, the risk of an aging, under-protected roof is very real. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has spent years helping Roosevelt Gardens homeowners understand exactly what's at stake — and what it takes to truly protect a home in this part of Broward County.

Located just 4.5 miles from the coast and sitting squarely within Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Roosevelt Gardens faces some of the most demanding roofing requirements in the entire state of Florida. Wind speeds in this zone are engineered to 170 mph, which means the roofing system above your head must be specifically tested, rated, and installed to meet Florida's strictest building standards. For homeowners near Roosevelt Gardens Park and throughout the surrounding streets, this isn't just bureaucratic fine print — it's the difference between a roof that survives a storm and one that leaves you filing an insurance claim or, worse, living without shelter. Every roofing project we complete here is designed with these HVHZ realities front and center.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the expertise, the products, and the local knowledge to deliver roofing solutions that are built for Roosevelt Gardens specifically — not generic installations that could work anywhere. We've completed hundreds of roofing projects across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand the nuances of working with older home structures common in Roosevelt Gardens, navigating permits through the Broward County Building Division, and selecting materials that hold up under the punishment South Florida weather delivers season after season. Whether you need a full roof replacement or targeted storm repairs, we're here to keep your home protected for decades to come.

section_why.h2: Why Roosevelt Gardens Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Every Roofing Project
section_why.body:
When it comes to choosing a roofing contractor in Roosevelt Gardens, there's no shortage of companies claiming to be the best — but very few can back it up with the credentials, track record, and local expertise that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings to every job. We're a licensed and insured contractor carrying an A+ BBB rating, and we've successfully completed 500+ homes across South Florida. That experience isn't just a number — it translates into smoother permit processes, fewer installation surprises, and roofs that are done right the first time.

One of the most important distinctions that sets us apart in Roosevelt Gardens is our commitment to 100% in-house installation. We do not subcontract our roofing crews. Every technician who touches your roof is a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee with hands-on training, company accountability, and a stake in the quality of the finished product. Many roofing companies in Broward County outsource their labor to third-party crews who may lack HVHZ-specific experience or proper licensing. When your home is in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone and you're counting on that roof to perform during a Category 4 storm, who installs your roof matters just as much as what's being installed.

Our team brings more than 60 years of combined experience to every project, and a significant portion of that experience has been earned right here in Broward County neighborhoods like Roosevelt Gardens, neighboring Fort Lauderdale, Lauderhill, and Lauderdale Lakes. We understand the older home construction styles common in this community, including the framing, decking conditions, and structural quirks often found in pre-1994 builds. That knowledge allows us to identify hidden issues during tear-off — rotted decking, improper flashing, failed underlayment — before they become expensive problems. Transparency is part of how we operate, and we'll walk you through every finding before any additional work begins.

From your first call through final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is your single point of contact. Our team handles every phase of the project: material selection, permit filing with the Broward County Building Division, installation, inspection scheduling, and post-project cleanup. We treat Roosevelt Gardens homeowners the same way we'd want our own families treated — with honesty, clear communication, and craftsmanship that lasts.

section_history.h2: Storms That Shaped Roosevelt Gardens: Why Your Roof Can't Afford to Fall Short
section_history.body:
Roosevelt Gardens and the surrounding Broward County communities have faced no fewer than 10 named storms since 1990, and the damage those storms left behind tells a story that every homeowner in this ZIP code should understand. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for South Florida roofing standards — the catastrophic destruction it caused across the region exposed just how unprepared most homes were for sustained high-wind events. Although Andrew's worst impacts were concentrated further south in Miami-Dade, Broward County communities including Roosevelt Gardens saw significant roof damage, and the aftermath directly shaped the modern Florida Building Code standards that govern HVHZ roofing today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of destruction to Broward County — fast-moving but fierce, with sustained winds and a rapid pressure drop that caught many homeowners off guard. Wilma peeled back thousands of roofs across the county, disproportionately damaging homes with aging shingles, improper nail patterns, and outdated underlayment systems. Roosevelt Gardens, with its high concentration of pre-1994 construction, felt that impact acutely. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which reinforced what many homeowners had already suspected: a roof that passed a basic inspection wasn't necessarily a roof that could handle a direct storm event. Irma's prolonged wind field caused extensive shingle loss and water intrusion in communities just like Roosevelt Gardens.

The pattern across all three major storms is clear — homes with older, non-HVHZ-compliant roofing systems suffered the most. That's precisely why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing focuses on installing fully rated, code-compliant roofing systems for every Roosevelt Gardens client. We're not just replacing your roof; we're bringing it up to the standard that modern storm science and Florida Building Code demand.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Roofing Codes in Broward County: What Roosevelt Gardens Homeowners Must Know
section_code.body:
Roosevelt Gardens falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation that carries the most stringent roofing requirements of any jurisdiction in the United States. The Florida Building Code, in its current edition enforced for Broward County, mandates that all roofing systems installed in HVHZ areas must be tested and approved under Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) protocols — a standard so rigorous that products must withstand simulated wind, rain, and debris impact before they're eligible for use. This is non-negotiable, and it's why not every roofing product sold at national hardware stores qualifies for installation in Roosevelt Gardens.

For homeowners in the 33311 ZIP code, this means every roof replacement must be designed to perform in wind zones rated at 170 mph. That requirement affects not just the shingles or tiles themselves, but the entire roofing system — including the decking attachment method, the underlayment selection, the fastener schedule, and the flashing details at every penetration, valley, and edge. All of this must be documented and submitted to the Broward County Building Division for permit approval before work begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, ensuring that your project is in full compliance from the first nail to the final inspection.

It's also worth noting that a permitted, code-compliant roof replacement in Broward County triggers a required wind mitigation inspection upon completion. This inspection documents the features of your new roof — nail pattern, roof cover type, roof deck attachment — and the resulting report can be submitted to your homeowner's insurance carrier for substantial premium discounts. Skipping the permit to save time or money isn't just illegal; it's a decision that costs Roosevelt Gardens homeowners in insurance savings, resale value, and long-term structural protection.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed & West Lake Roofing Systems Built for Roosevelt Gardens' Toughest Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing product lines that are purpose-built for South Florida's demanding environment: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both brands are rigorously tested for HVHZ compliance and carry the Miami-Dade NOA approvals required for installation in Roosevelt Gardens and throughout Broward County. Selecting the right product for your home depends on your budget, your home's structural design, and your aesthetic goals — and our team will help you make that decision with complete confidence.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles represent one of the most respected shingle products available in the Florida market. These are not standard three-tab shingles — they're dimensional, dual-layered shingles engineered to resist uplift in high-wind environments while delivering the curb appeal of a premium roof. The Landmark Series carries a Class A fire rating, a strong wind warranty, and the backing of CertainTeed's SureStart PLUS warranty program when installed by certified contractors like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. For Roosevelt Gardens homeowners looking for a high-performance roof replacement that fits a practical budget, CertainTeed Landmark shingles are an outstanding choice that blends durability with value.

For homeowners interested in concrete or clay tile roofing, West Lake offers a comprehensive lineup of products that bring both exceptional longevity and classic South Florida aesthetics to any home. Tile roofs installed with West Lake systems are among the most durable options available — capable of lasting 40 to 50 years with proper maintenance — and they carry an inherent resistance to wind, moisture, and UV degradation that makes them especially well-suited to the Broward County climate. Whether your Roosevelt Gardens home calls for the look of traditional barrel tile or a flat concrete profile, West Lake's HVHZ-rated systems give you a roof that is as beautiful as it is battle-tested.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Costs in Roosevelt Gardens: Honest Numbers and Smart Financing Options
section_cost.body:
For Roosevelt Gardens homeowners, understanding the true cost of a roof replacement is essential to making a confident, informed decision. In Broward County's HVHZ, roofing costs reflect the higher material standards and labor requirements mandated by the Florida Building Code. For a typical single-family home in the Roosevelt Gardens area — averaging around $240,000 in value — a full roof replacement using CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingles generally ranges from $12,000 to $18,000 depending on roof size, pitch, existing condition, and the complexity of the installation. Tile roofing with West Lake systems typically carries a higher starting investment, reflecting the materials and additional labor involved, but the long-term payoff in durability and insurance savings can be substantial.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing believes that every Roosevelt Gardens homeowner deserves access to quality roofing regardless of their current financial situation. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. A new roof is one of the highest-ROI home improvements you can make in South Florida — protecting your home's structural integrity, increasing resale value, and dramatically reducing your exposure to post-storm repair costs. When you factor in the insurance savings a new, wind-mitigated roof can generate, many Roosevelt Gardens homeowners find that the monthly financing cost is partially or fully offset within the first year.

We also encourage homeowners to think about roofing investment in the context of what deferred replacement actually costs. An aging, failing roof in an HVHZ environment creates compounding risks: interior water damage, mold growth, compromised insulation, and potential claim denials if your carrier determines the roof was already at end of life before a storm hit. Getting ahead of those costs with a planned replacement — financed affordably through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — is almost always the smarter financial decision compared to emergency repairs after damage occurs.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Roosevelt Gardens Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premium
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially meaningful benefits of a properly permitted, HVHZ-compliant roof replacement in Roosevelt Gardens is the impact it can have on your homeowner's insurance premium. Florida carriers — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance — offer significant wind mitigation credits to policyholders who can document specific roof features through a certified wind mitigation inspection. These credits are calculated based on your roof deck attachment method, the type of roof cover installed, and whether your roof-to-wall connections meet current standards. A new CertainTeed or West Lake roofing system installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing checks every one of those boxes.

The savings can be substantial. Broward County homeowners who replace aging, non-compliant roofs with modern HVHZ-rated systems often see homeowner's insurance premium reductions ranging from 15% to 30% annually — sometimes more, depending on the carrier and the specific features of the new roof. On a policy that runs $4,000 to $6,000 per year — which is increasingly common in South Florida — that represents hundreds or even thousands of dollars in annual savings. Over the life of a new roof, those cumulative savings can offset a significant portion of the initial replacement investment.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing strongly recommends that every Roosevelt Gardens homeowner schedule a wind mitigation inspection immediately following their new roof installation. We'll coordinate the scheduling for you and ensure your project documentation is complete and organized so the inspector has everything needed to certify the full scope of your new roof's protective features. We can also help you communicate the results to your insurance agent to ensure you're receiving every credit you're entitled to. It's one more way Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers value that extends far beyond the day your new roof is completed.

faqs.q: Does my Roosevelt Gardens home really need an HVHZ-rated roof, and what happens if I use non-compliant materials?
faqs.a: Yes — Roosevelt Gardens falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which legally requires all roofing systems to carry Miami-Dade NOA approval. Using non-compliant materials means your roof won't pass the required Broward County Building Division inspection, your permit will be rejected, and your insurance company may deny storm claims if they discover the installation didn't meet code. HVHZ compliance isn't optional — it's the law and your best protection.

faqs.q: What does a full roof replacement cost in Roosevelt Gardens, and what financing does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offer?
faqs.a: In Roosevelt Gardens, a full CertainTeed shingle roof replacement typically ranges from $12,000 to $18,000 depending on home size and roof complexity. West Lake tile systems are priced higher due to materials and labor requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants, making it possible to get a hurricane-rated, code-compliant roof without a large upfront payment.

faqs.q: How does the roofing permit process work in Broward County, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle it?
faqs.a: All roof replacements in Roosevelt Gardens require a permit from the Broward County Building Division, along with product approval documentation confirming HVHZ compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process for you — from preparing and submitting the permit application to scheduling the required inspections. We ensure your project is fully compliant at every stage so you never have to worry about code violations, failed inspections, or delays.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical Roosevelt Gardens home?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements in Roosevelt Gardens are completed within one to three days for shingle systems, with tile roofs sometimes requiring an additional day depending on the size and complexity of the home. Permit approval timelines through the Broward County Building Division can add several business days before installation begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire schedule and keeps you informed at every step so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new HVHZ-rated roof installed with proper nail patterns, code-compliant underlayment, and approved roof covering qualifies for wind mitigation credits through Florida carriers like Citizens, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance. Roosevelt Gardens homeowners frequently see annual premium reductions of 15% to 30% after a roof replacement and wind mitigation inspection, which can add up to thousands of dollars in savings over the life of the roof.

Contact us today for your free roof inspection and estimate.
